history.text: Compare the weather in three cities I'm considering moving to: Austin, Denver, and Portland

Austin features a humid subtropical climate with hot, humid summers often exceeding 90°F and mild winters, though spring and fall can see significant rain. Denver boasts a high desert, semi-arid climate characterized by abundant sunshine year-round, cool summers, and cold, snowy winters due to its high elevation; it is notably dry with distinct seasonal temperature swings. Portland has a marine west coast climate with mild, wet winters featuring frequent drizzle and fog, coupled with warm, dry summers, making it one of the wettest cities in the contiguous US. Essentially, if you prefer dry heat, choose Austin or Denver, while Portland suits those who don't mind consistent precipitation in exchange for mild temperatures throughout the year.
